sections.a_eyebrow: Modular Layout · Grid · Card Layout · Modern · Professional
sections.a_description: The distinctive visual language of Bento Box Layout — combining modular layout, grid, card layout elements to create interfaces that are immediately recognizable and purposefully designed. Every color, font choice, and spacing decision works together to evoke the specific mood and cultural context that defines this aesthetic, from the primary accent color to the carefully chosen typography.

This style finds its natural home across websites and applications that value modular layout and grid design principles. You'll see it on creative portfolios, brand microsites, specialized platforms, and digital experiences where the visual language itself communicates as powerfully as the content — proving that the right aesthetic choice can transform a standard layout into a memorable experience.

a_image_cover.alt: Bento Box Layout style use case illustration
Bento Box Layout style use case illustration
Bento Box Layout style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The distinctive visual aesthetic of Bento Box Layout — a design approach that creates unique, memorable interfaces. Background: light (#f5f5f7). The .inner panel has a clean background with subtle border treatment and appropriate border-radius. Colors: #111111 as the primary accent for interactive elements and highlights. #1d1d1f for headings, #6e6e73 for body text. The aesthetic captures the essence of bento box layout through careful color choices, spacing, and visual weight. Set in SF Pro Display — chosen for its visual character that complements this design style. Heading at weight 600-700, 24-28px, line-height 1.2-1.3. Eyebrow at weight 500, 11px, uppercase, 2-3px letter-spacing, color #111111. Body at weight 400, 14-15px, line-height 1.6-1.7, color #6e6e73. Buttons: primary — #111111 background with contrasting text. Secondary: transparent with #111111 border. Alternatives: system-ui (universal fallback), sans-serif stack.
